entries.content:
Well, there we go, then—no worse than brushing one's fingertips across the electric hob when tidying up after someone who never wipes it down, is it? No reason for dismay; simply press on, lips tightened with polite resolve. It's best not to dwell on little pains—after all, fuss is the ruin of a tranquil household, or so we’re told, though one might wonder just who profits from that silence.

entries.content:
Just a little tingle this time—nothing compared to the thousand daily pinpricks women bear with a gracious smile. Carry on with composure; a tidy emotional response is the secret ingredient in any well-run experiment, or home. Best to distract oneself with thoughts of clean linens or daffodils on the sideboard, and continue as required.
